target.title: docs(tools): document owner-only tools and commands.ownerAllowFrom
target.body:
## Summary

Add documentation for owner-only tools and `commands.ownerAllowFrom` configuration, addressing #53058.

### Problem

Several tools (`gateway`, `cron`, `nodes`) are gated behind `ownerOnly: true` in source but this isn't documented anywhere. Users with `tools.profile: "full"` are confused when agents can't access these tools and silently fall back to CLI workarounds. The only way to discover this was reading source code.

### Changes

Added an "Owner-only tools" section to `docs/tools/index.md` covering:

- **Which tools require ownership** — `gateway`, `cron`, `nodes` listed in a table
- **How to configure** — `commands.ownerAllowFrom` with channel-native sender IDs
- **Examples** — Discord, WhatsApp, Telegram ID formats
- **How to find your sender ID** — gateway logs and Discord Developer Mode
- **Troubleshooting** — explains the silent fallback behavior

### Source references

- `src/agents/tool-policy.ts` — owner filtering logic
- `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool.ts`, `cron-tool.ts`, `nodes-tool.ts` — `ownerOnly: true`
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — existing but undocumented help string

Closes #53058

target.title: docs(skills): add advanced features section to creating-skills guide
target.body:
## Summary

Add an "Advanced features" section to `docs/tools/creating-skills.md` that bridges the gap between the basic getting-started guide and the full skills reference.

### Problem

As reported in #39681, the creating-skills guide only covers a bare-bones hello-world example. Critical skill authoring features (conditional activation, API key injection, multi-command dispatch, invocation control, template variables) are only discoverable by reading the full `skills.md` reference — or never discovered at all.

### Changes

Added a new "Advanced features" section covering:

- **Conditional activation** — `requires.bins`, `requires.env`, `requires.config`
- **API key and env injection** — `skills.entries.<name>.apiKey` / `.env`
- **Invocation control** — `user-invocable`, `disable-model-invocation`
- **Multi-command skills** — `command-dispatch: tool` with `command-tool`
- **Template variables** — `{baseDir}`
- **Publishing to ClawHub**

Each item links to the relevant section in the skills reference for details.

Closes #39681

target.title: feat(android): Add message copy and text selection to chat screen
target.body: # PR: Enable Text Interaction via ModalBottomSheet (Closes #57754) ## Summary - Problem: Users were unable to copy, share, or quote standard chat message text on the Android app (only code blocks were selectable), causing significant friction for mobile users. - Why it matters: Mobile users frequently need to extract, share, or reply to specific parts of AI responses. Switching to a desktop just to copy text is a broken UX. - What changed: Implemented a native Material 3 `ModalBottomSheet` triggered by a long-press on chat bubbles, offering 4 core actions: Copy, Select Copy (via a dedicated `SelectionContainer` dialog), Share, and Reply. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Core message markdown rendering logic, backend API contracts, and project build files (AGP/Java toolchain changes were intentionally kept out of this PR to avoid CI/CD breaks). --- ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra --- ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra --- ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #57754 - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression --- ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) N/A --- ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) N/A --- ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **New Interaction:** Long-pressing any chat bubble now slides up a native Bottom Sheet instead of doing nothing. - **New Actions:** Users can now tap: - **Copy** → copies entire text to clipboard - **Select Copy** → opens a scrollable dialog for precise selection - **Share** → opens Android system share sheet - **Reply** → quotes the message in the composer --- ## Diagram ```text Before: [Long Press on Chat Bubble] -> [No Action / Only code blocks selectable] -> [User frustration] After: [Long Press on Chat Bubble] -> [ModalBottomSheet Opens] ├─> [Tap 'Copy'] -> [Text in Clipboard] ├─> [Tap 'Select Copy'] -> [Selection Dialog Opens] -> [Native Text Selection] ├─> [Tap 'Share'] -> [Android Share Sheet Opens] └─> [Tap 'Reply'] -> [Message Quoted in Composer] ``` --- ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A --- ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Android (Physical Device / Emulator) - Runtime/container: Android OS (Jetpack Compose UI) - Model/provider: N/A (UI layer) - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Launch the OpenClaw Android app and open any chat thread. 2. Long-press on a standard text message bubble. 3. Observe the `ModalBottomSheet` sliding up with 4 actions. 4. Tap 'Select Copy' to verify the `SelectionContainer` dialog opens and allows precise text selection. ### Expected - Bottom sheet opens smoothly without interfering with the `LazyColumn` scroll state. - All 4 actions execute their respective device-level intents natively. ### Actual - Bottom sheet opens as expected. - 'Select Copy' dialog scales properly based on content size up to a maximum height. --- ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) **Screenshots:** - - - --- ## Human Verification (required) - **Verified scenarios:** Tested all 4 actions (Copy, Select Copy, Share, Reply) on long and short messages. Verified that the Reply action properly populates the chat composer state. - **Edge cases checked:** - Very long text handled using `.heightIn(max = 400.dp)` - Prevented crashes using `?.let` instead of `!!` - **Not verified:** End-to-end integration with live WebSocket messages (tested with local UI state). --- ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. --- ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` --- ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk:** Unnecessary recompositions of the `LazyColumn` when bottom sheet state changes. - **Mitigation:** - State is hoisted using `remember` at bubble level - Used `?.let` for safe rendering - Scoped recomposition only to interacted component

target.title: feat(cron): show before/after diff in `cron edit` before applying changes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `cron edit` applied changes silently with no preview, making it easy to accidentally misconfigure a job without realising until the next run.
- Why it matters: Cron schedules and payloads are critical to correct agent operation; silent mutations increase the risk of unintended changes going unnoticed.
- What changed: Added a before/after diff preview to `cron edit`. The diff is sent to stderr so it doesn't corrupt `--json` output. Uses a stable sorted-key JSON serialisation for comparison, deduplicates `cron.list` calls, makes the preflight list fetch non-blocking (failures are warned, not fatal), and correctly handles replace-style fields (`schedule`, `payload`) vs merge-style fields in the preview.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Actual update logic (`cron.update` RPC) is unchanged. The diff is informational only; it does not gate the update.

## Change Type (select all)
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #59453
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)
- Root cause: N/A – new feature, not a regression.
- Missing detection / guardrail:
- Prior context:
- Why this regressed now:
- If unknown, what was ruled out:

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cli/cron-cli/`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Preview shows correct before/after for schedule and payload fields; sorted-key serialisation eliminates key-order noise.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Unit tests on `computeDisplayAfter*` functions with mock job data.
- Existing test that already covers this: N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — unit tests cover the preview logic.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`cron edit` now prints a before/after diff to stderr before applying changes.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: `cron edit <id> --every 2h` shows before/after diff on stderr; actual job is updated correctly. `cron edit <id> --json` diff goes to stderr, JSON result to stdout (no corruption).
- Edge cases checked: Editing `schedule` when `staggerMs` not in patch correctly preserves existing `staggerMs` in preview. Editing `payload` with same `kind` shows field-level merge preview.
- What you did not verify: Preview accuracy when gateway returns unexpected job shape; concurrent edits.

## Review Conversations
- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

---

## Summary

Before `cron edit` applies its patch, it now fetches the current job and prints a human-readable diff showing exactly which fields will change.

## Before / After

```
$ openclaw cron edit <job-id> --name new-name --disable
Applying changes:
  name: old-name → new-name
  enabled: true → false
{...updated job JSON...}
```

## Behavior

- Fetches current job via `cron.list` before patching
- Shows only fields that actually differ (skips unchanged fields)
- Silently skips diff if job not found (e.g. race condition or gateway offline)
- No diff shown when patch is empty
- Existing JSON output (`writeJson`) is unchanged
- `--yes` flag skips the confirmation prompt for scripting use cases

## Testing

- Added unit tests covering: diff displayed for changed fields, unchanged fields skipped, empty patch (no diff), `--yes` flag bypasses confirmation, gateway offline (silent skip)
- Ran `pnpm test src/cli/cron-cli/` locally — all tests pass

## Related

Closes #59453

target.title: feat: add context visualization in tui
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The `/context` command in the TUI only outputs static, text-based context breakdowns with no interactivity or visual representation of context composition.
- Why it matters: Understanding context usage and growth is critical for debugging prompt engineering, identifying oversized bootstrap files, and monitoring token utilization during sessions.
- What changed: Added an interactive TUI overlay invoked via `/context visualize` (or `/context viz`) that renders color-coded bar charts, a proportional stacked bar, a growth sparkline, and drill-down views for each context category (system prompt, workspace files, skills, tools).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): The existing `/context`, `/context list`, `/context detail`, and `/context json` gateway command behavior is untouched. No new gateway endpoints were added; the overlay reuses the existing `sessions.usage` endpoint with `includeContextWeight: true`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # N/A
- Related # N/A
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `/context visualize` (or `/context viz`) in the TUI opens an interactive overlay with color-coded bar charts, a growth sparkline, and token utilization percentage.
- Keyboard navigation: `j/k` or arrows to navigate categories, `Enter` to drill into a category's items, `Esc`/`Backspace` to go back, `r` to refresh, `q` to close.
- Live auto-refresh every 2 seconds while the overlay is open.
- `/context` now appears in slash command autocomplete with subcommand completions (`visualize`, `viz`, `list`, `detail`, `json`).
- Bare `/context` and existing subcommands (`list`, `detail`, `json`) continue to forward to the gateway as before.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
/context [any] -> gateway message -> static text reply

After:
/context visualize|viz -> local TUI overlay (interactive, live-updating)
/context [list|detail|json|help|<empty>] -> gateway message -> static text reply (unchanged)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` (reuses existing `sessions.usage` endpoint)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / Bun
- Model/provider: Any
- Integration/channel (if any): TUI

### Steps

1. Run `pnpm dev` to start the TUI
2. Type `/context viz` and press Enter
3. Use `j/k` to navigate categories, `Enter` to drill into one
4. Press `Esc` to return to overview, `q` or `Esc` to close
5. Verify `/context list` still returns static text from gateway

### Expected

- Interactive overlay renders with bar charts, sparkline, and token utilization
- Drill-down shows per-item breakdowns (files, skills, tools)
- Overlay auto-refreshes every 2s with visible repaint
- Existing `/context list|detail|json` subcommands work unchanged

### Actual

- (To be verified manually)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

All 202 TUI tests pass (`pnpm test -- src/tui/`). Updated 5 test cases in `tui-command-handlers.test.ts` that used `/context` as an "unknown command" example (changed to `/foobar`). TypeScript type-check passes (`pnpm tsgo`) with no new errors.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: TUI test suite (202 tests, 18 files pass), type-check, format/lint
- Edge cases checked: Updated tests that relied on `/context` being an unknown command; verified `/context` without args still forwards to gateway
- What you did **not** verify: Manual TUI overlay rendering (requires running gateway)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The 2-second polling interval could add unnecessary gateway load if many TUI clients leave the overlay open.
  - Mitigation: Polling only runs while the overlay is open and stops on close via `clearInterval`. The `sessions.usage` endpoint is lightweight and already exists.

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared. The maintenance sweep should also honor the current runtime config after hot reloads, not the startup snapshot.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed. The initial scheduler also captured the startup config, which meant hot reloads could leave the sweep using stale reset policy until restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - A stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message.
  - A fresh daily session is left alone.
  - Implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are not cut by the default daily policy.
  - Sessions with active runs are skipped.
  - Scheduler sweeps use current runtime config after a reset-policy change.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution or reload-aware maintenance behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary. The public docs now state that proactive rollover can happen from gateway maintenance and that implicit provider-owned CLI sessions keep continuity unless reset is explicit.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> read current runtime config
  -> skip active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.
5. Mark a stale session key active and confirm it is skipped.
6. Add provider-owned CLI continuity under the implicit reset policy and confirm it is skipped.
7. Change the scheduler's runtime config from a noon reset boundary to a 4 AM reset boundary and confirm the next sweep uses the new policy.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh, active, and implicitly provider-owned CLI sessions remain unchanged; config reloads affect future scheduler sweeps.

### Actual

The focused scheduler and gateway maintenance tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused scheduler and maintenance test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.
- Docs updated in:
  - `docs/concepts/session.md`
  - `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`
  - `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, runtime-config reload path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer; type gates pass.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
- Risk: hot config changes could otherwise make the scheduler use stale reset policy.
- Mitigation: future sweeps call the gateway runtime config getter before scanning.

target.title: fix: drop replayed tool-call stubs from malformed assistant history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: provider-switched or malformed assistant transcript entries can replay literal `[TOOL CALL STUB]` placeholder text back into context.
- Why it matters: those stub-only turns are dead transcript artifacts, not meaningful memory, and they can pollute replayed context after provider changes.
- What changed: `sanitizeSessionHistory` now strips tool-call stub placeholders from malformed assistant string content and drops assistant entries that are stub-only after cleanup.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change transcript storage format, provider translation, or tool-call/result pairing rules for valid structured history.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: malformed assistant history entries with string `content` were canonicalized into plain text replay messages without filtering known transcript placeholder artifacts.
- Missing detection / guardrail: replay sanitization covered malformed assistant content shape, but not the specific case where that malformed content was only a tool-call placeholder.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): current replay sanitizer already canonicalizes malformed assistant content in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/google.ts`, and existing tests cover that generic path plus provider-switch replay edge cases.
- Why this regressed now: provider switching can leave transcript artifacts that are valid strings structurally, so they bypassed stricter tool-call normalization and survived as replayable text.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: this fix does not rely on changing persisted session format or provider-specific tool ID logic; existing OpenAI tool ID preservation coverage still passes.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: malformed assistant string content containing `[TOOL CALL STUB]` is either dropped when stub-only or preserved as prose with the placeholder removed.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior lives entirely inside replay sanitization and does not require provider IO or session file rewriting to reproduce.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.openai-tool-id-preservation.test.ts` was rerun to confirm adjacent replay behavior still holds.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users no longer get replay context polluted by literal `[TOOL CALL STUB]` assistant history placeholders after malformed/provider-switched transcript history is sanitized.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[malformed assistant string: "[TOOL CALL STUB]"] -> canonicalized to replayable text block

After:
[malformed assistant string: "[TOOL CALL STUB]"] -> recognized as placeholder-only -> dropped
[malformed assistant string: "Before [TOOL CALL STUB] after"] -> placeholder stripped -> "Before after"
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node/Vitest
- Model/provider: replay sanitizer coverage for `openai` plus adjacent OpenAI replay regression
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): default unit test harness

### Steps

1. Create malformed assistant replay history with string content equal to `[TOOL CALL STUB]`.
2. Run `sanitizeSessionHistory(...)` for replay.
3. Repeat with mixed prose plus placeholder text.

### Expected

- Stub-only assistant history is not replayed.
- Mixed prose remains replayable, but the placeholder is removed.

### Actual

- Before this change, malformed assistant string content was canonicalized directly into replay text, preserving the placeholder artifact.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: targeted replay sanitizer test suite and adjacent OpenAI tool ID preservation regression test.
- Edge cases checked: stub-only placeholder entry, prose surrounding placeholder, existing OpenAI replay ID preservation.
- What you did **not** verify: full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` repo-wide lane.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: placeholder stripping could remove literal user-authored text if an assistant malformed history string intentionally contains `[TOOL CALL STUB]`.
  - Mitigation: the change is limited to malformed assistant replay entries already on the canonicalization fallback path, and the cleanup only removes the specific placeholder token.

target.title: fix(skills): restore executable bit on bundled whisper script + release-time check
target.body:
## Problem

The bundled Whisper skill shell script (`skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh`) lost its executable bit at some point, causing `EACCES` failures when the skill tries to run it (fixes #9303).

## Solution

- Restore the executable bit (`chmod +x`) on `transcribe.sh`
- Add a release-time check (`collectSkillShellScriptExecutableErrors`) that scans all `skills/*/scripts/*.sh` files and fails the build if any are missing the executable bit — prevents this class of regression permanently

## Testing

- 10 tests pass including 2 new tests for the executable-bit checker (non-executable detection + executable acceptance)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Bundled OpenAI Whisper and video-frame skill helper scripts must be executable so direct helper invocation does not fail with EACCES.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head 2204a78702 on macOS with bash/zsh, Node, curl, and Homebrew ffmpeg. The Whisper request used a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP stub so no audio or API key left the machine.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Verified git modes and execute bits, ran `skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh` against the local stub, generated a tiny video with ffmpeg, then ran `skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh` to extract a frame.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the PR checkout:

```text
$ git rev-parse --short HEAD
2204a78702
$ git ls-tree HEAD skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh
100755 blob 2a4fd20afa6104f0624a6f60ed82e4a81671ec1d	skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh
100755 blob 31b3adb34c068732d04cb0d951d519338458df98	skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh
$ test -x skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh && test -x skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh && echo executable
executable
$ OPENAI_API_KEY=redacted OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:18743/v1 skills/openai-whisper-api/scripts/transcribe.sh $tmp/sample.wav --out $tmp/transcript.txt
/var/folders/wk/9gjf5c_940g4npdlmg0r3h8c0000gn/T/tmp.9p5hDDgFuQ/transcript.txt
$ sed -n '1p' $tmp/transcript.txt
transcribed by local proof endpoint
$ command -v ffmpeg
/opt/homebrew/bin/ffmpeg
$ ffmpeg -hide_banner -loglevel error -y -f lavfi -i testsrc=size=32x32:rate=1:duration=1 $tmp/sample.mp4
$ skills/video-frames/scripts/frame.sh $tmp/sample.mp4 --index 0 --out $tmp/frame.jpg
/var/folders/wk/9gjf5c_940g4npdlmg0r3h8c0000gn/T/tmp.9p5hDDgFuQ/frame.jpg
$ file $tmp/frame.jpg
/var/folders/wk/9gjf5c_940g4npdlmg0r3h8c0000gn/T/tmp.9p5hDDgFuQ/frame.jpg: JPEG image data, JFIF standard 1.02, aspect ratio, density 1x1, segment length 16, comment: "Lavc62.11.100", baseline, precision 8, 32x32, components 3
$ test -s $tmp/frame.jpg && echo frame-created
frame-created
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Both helper scripts are recorded as 100755 and pass `test -x`; the Whisper helper wrote the expected transcript text from the local endpoint; the video-frame helper wrote a non-empty 32x32 JPEG frame.
- **What was not tested:** A live OpenAI API transcription request was not sent; the local OpenAI-compatible endpoint was used to exercise the helper's direct execution and upload path without external data transfer.

target.title: Avoid config fallback for channels status probe failures
target.body:
## Summary

Avoid falling back to config-only status when `channels status --probe` fails after reaching the gateway.

## What changed

- Updated `src/commands/channels/status.ts` so probe-mode failures are rethrown instead of being treated as generic gateway-unreachable failures
- Kept the existing config-only fallback behavior for non-probe failures
- Added a focused regression test covering probe-mode failure after the gateway path is reached

## Why

`channels status --probe` previously treated any `callGateway(...)` failure as if the gateway itself were unreachable. That caused probe-specific failures to be reported through the generic config-only fallback path, which is misleading when the command has already reached the gateway and only the probe step failed.

This change preserves the original probe failure instead of misreporting it as a gateway reachability problem.

## Scope boundary

This PR only changes probe-mode failure handling in `channels status`. It does not change the normal config-only fallback behavior for non-probe failures.

## Manual verification

1. Added a focused test in `src/commands/channels.status.command-flow.test.ts`
2. Verified that probe-mode failures now rethrow the original error instead of falling back to config-only status
3. Ran:
   - `pnpm vitest run src/commands/channels.status.command-flow.test.ts -t "does not fall back to config-only status when probe mode fails after reaching the gateway"`
   - `pnpm vitest run src/commands/channels.status.command-flow.test.ts`
4. Ran the repo checks during commit

target.title: feat(doctor): add Node.js runtime info health contribution
target.body:
## Summary

Add a new Doctor health contribution that displays Node.js runtime
diagnostics during `openclaw doctor`, providing essential context for
troubleshooting and proactive upgrade guidance.

## What it does

**Runtime summary note** (always shown):
Node 24.14.0 · ~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node · via nvm

**Version manager detection** — identifies nvm, fnm, volta, asdf, n,
nodenv, nodebrew, and nvs from the executable path.

**Proactive warnings** (when applicable):
- Node version below minimum (>=22.14.0) — rarely triggers since the
  startup gate catches this first, but guards edge cases
- Node release in maintenance phase — shows months remaining until EOL
- Node release past end-of-life — recommends upgrading to Node 24
- Older-than-recommended LTS — gentle nudge toward Node 24

## Why

Doctor currently has no visibility into the Node.js runtime environment.
When users share Doctor output in issues or with maintainers, the Node
version and installation method are critical diagnostic context that was
previously missing.

This reuses existing infrastructure:
- `runtime-guard.ts` — `detectRuntime()`, `parseSemver()`, `runtimeSatisfies()`
- `runtime-paths.ts` — `isVersionManagedNodePath()`

No new dependencies. No runtime behavior changes outside of Doctor.

## Changes

New files:
- `src/commands/doctor-node-runtime.ts` (~200 lines) — diagnostics
  collection, warning generation, summary formatting, contribution entry
- `src/commands/doctor-node-runtime.test.ts` — 27 unit tests

Modified:
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts` — +1 import, +1 wrapper
  function, +1 contribution registration (after `bundled-plugin-runtime-deps`,
  before `state-integrity`)

## Test plan

- `pnpm check` — 0 errors ✅
- `vitest` — 27/27 tests ✅
- `pnpm build` — clean ✅

Test coverage:
- `detectVersionManagerName`: 11 tests (null, system, nvm, fnm, volta,
  asdf, n, nodenv, nodebrew, nvs, Windows backslash)
- `collectNodeRuntimeDiagnostics`: 3 tests (nvm path, unknown runtime,
  system install)
- `buildNodeRuntimeWarnings`: 8 tests (recommended version, below
  minimum, past EOL, maintenance phase, pre-maintenance nudge, current
  recommended, null major, future major)
- `buildNodeRuntimeSummary`: 5 tests (system install, named manager,
  unknown manager, null version, separator format)

## Summary

Validate `nodes` CLI timeout values using the shared timeout parsing helper.

## What changed

- Replaced raw `Number(opts.timeout ?? 10_000)` parsing in `src/cli/nodes-cli/rpc.ts`
- Switched the nodes CLI transport timeout path to `parseTimeoutMsWithFallback(..., { invalidType: "error" })`

## Why

The nodes CLI RPC path was still parsing `--timeout` with a raw `Number(...)` conversion, unlike other CLI paths that already use the shared timeout parsing helper.

This made invalid values less consistently validated. This change brings the nodes CLI timeout behavior in line with the existing shared timeout parsing rules and makes invalid values like `0` fail clearly.

target.title: feat(doctor): detect volatile filesystem (tmpfs/ramfs/overlay) for state directory
target.body:
## Summary

Add detection for state directories on volatile filesystems (tmpfs, ramfs, overlay) that lose all data on reboot. This complements the existing SD/eMMC storage and iCloud cloud-sync detection in `doctor-state-integrity`.

## Problem

Container deployments (Docker, LXC) and certain Linux configurations may place the state directory on tmpfs, ramfs, or an overlay root filesystem. When this happens, sessions, credentials, and config are silently destroyed on every reboot. Doctor currently checks for cloud-synced dirs (macOS) and SD/eMMC media (Linux) but has no detection for volatile filesystems.

## Solution

Reuse the existing `/proc/self/mountinfo` parsing infrastructure (`parseLinuxMountInfo` + `findLinuxMountInfoEntryForPath`) to check whether the state directory's filesystem type is in the volatile set (`tmpfs`, `ramfs`, `overlay`).

### Modified: `src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts`
- `LinuxVolatileStateDir` type — mirrors `LinuxSdBackedStateDir` structure
- `VOLATILE_FS_TYPES` — `Set` of volatile filesystem type strings
- `detectLinuxVolatileStateDir()` — reuses mountinfo parsing, checks fsType against volatile set
- `formatLinuxVolatileStateDirWarning()` — follows existing warning format conventions
- `noteStateIntegrity()` — register volatile-fs warning between SD/eMMC and state-dir-exists checks

### New: `src/commands/doctor-volatile-fs.test.ts` (10 tests)
- tmpfs / ramfs / overlay detection
- ext4 non-detection (no false positive)
- Non-linux platform skip
- Null mountInfo handling
- Most-specific mount point selection
- No false positive when `/tmp` is tmpfs but state dir is on ext4
- Warning format validation

## Design decisions

1. **Reuse existing infrastructure** — `parseLinuxMountInfo` and `findLinuxMountInfoEntryForPath` already solve mount-point matching. No new parsing code needed.
2. **Same dependency injection pattern** — `deps?` parameter with `platform`, `mountInfo`, `resolveRealPath` for testability, consistent with `detectLinuxSdBackedStateDir`.
3. **overlay included** — Docker's default storage driver uses overlay2/overlay. A state directory on an overlay root without a volume mount will lose data.
4. **Warning placement** — after SD/eMMC check, before state-dir-exists check, so volatile-fs warning appears alongside other storage-quality warnings.

## Verification

- `pnpm check`: 0 errors
- `pnpm vitest run src/commands/doctor-volatile-fs.test.ts`: 10/10 passed

target.title: fix: Config Raw mode permanently disabled due to round-trip check regression
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #59330 — Control UI Config editor permanently shows "Raw mode disabled (snapshot cannot safely round-trip raw text)" since 2026.3.31.

## Root Cause

Two issues cause `shouldFallbackToStructuredRawRedaction()` to always return `true`:

### 1. `normalizeExecSafeBinProfilesInConfig` assigns `void 0` instead of deleting keys

When `safeBinProfiles`/`safeBinTrustedDirs` are empty, the normalize function assigns `undefined`:

```ts
typedExec.safeBinProfiles = ... ? normalizedProfiles : undefined;
```

This creates keys with `undefined` values. JSON-parsed objects can never have `undefined`-valued keys, so `isDeepStrictEqual()` always fails:

```ts
isDeepStrictEqual({ safeBinProfiles: undefined }, {}) // → false
```

### 2. `shouldFallbackToStructuredRawRedaction` compares against materialized config

The round-trip check compares the restored (raw-parsed) config against `snapshot.config` (the **materialized** runtime config), which contains transformations not present in the raw source:

- `~/` paths expanded to absolute paths
- Model `api` field inherited from provider level  
- Auto-generated `alias` fields on model entries
- Default empty `config: {}` added to plugin entries

The comparison should use `snapshot.parsed` (the source config before materialization).

## Changes

1. **`src/config/normalize-exec-safe-bin.ts`**: Use `delete` instead of assigning `undefined` when profiles/dirs are empty.

2. **`src/config/redact-snapshot.raw.ts`**: 
   - Accept optional `sourceConfig` parameter for comparison target
   - Normalize both sides with `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify())` to strip any remaining `undefined`-valued keys

3. **`src/config/redact-snapshot.ts`**: Pass `snapshot.parsed` as `sourceConfig` to the round-trip check.

## Testing

- Verified locally: `redactConfigSnapshot()` now returns `raw` as a string (not null)
- Raw mode is re-enabled in Control UI after these changes
- Confirmed the fix handles all materialization diffs (path expansion, field inheritance, alias generation, default objects)

---

🤖 AI-assisted (Claude via OpenClaw). Fully tested locally against real config. I understand what the code does.

target.title: feat(skills): notify agents of available updates via skill preambles
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: No way to know a newer OpenClaw version is available when working inside a Claude Code skill. Users must remember to run `openclaw update` manually.
- Why it matters: Stale versions mean missing bug fixes and features. Seamless upgrade notifications (like [gstack](https://github.com/garrytan/gstack) uses) keep users current without interrupting their workflow.
- What changed: Added `openclaw update hint` subcommand and bash preambles in all 7 `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md` files. The command reads cached `~/.openclaw/update-check.json` (zero network calls) and prints a one-liner when an update is available.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to the gateway update-check logic, no new network calls, no config schema changes. The existing `update-check.json` state file is consumed read-only.

Feature proposal: #59304

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #59304
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A — new feature.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — new feature. 13 unit tests cover all code paths.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New CLI subcommand: `openclaw update hint` — prints `UPGRADE_AVAILABLE <current> <latest> (<channel>). Run: openclaw update` when a newer version is available, silent otherwise.
- `openclaw update hint --json` — always emits a JSON object (`{ "updateAvailable": false }` or full update details).
- Every skill invocation now runs a 2-line preamble that surfaces the hint to the agent context.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[skill invocation] -> [skill runs] (no update awareness)

After:
[skill invocation] -> [preamble: openclaw update hint] -> [UPGRADE_AVAILABLE ...] -> [skill runs]
                                                       -> [silent if up-to-date]  -> [skill runs]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No (reads local cached file only)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes — new `openclaw update hint` subcommand
- Data access scope changed? No
- The new subcommand reads `~/.openclaw/update-check.json` (already written by the gateway). No secrets, no network, no side effects. The preamble runs with `2>/dev/null || true` to suppress errors.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.5 (Darwin 24.5.0)
- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Claude Code skills
- Relevant config (redacted): Default

### Steps

1. Run `openclaw update hint` with no `update-check.json` — expect silent (or `{ "updateAvailable": false }` with `--json`)
2. Create `~/.openclaw/update-check.json` with `{ "lastAvailableVersion": "2099.1.1" }` — run `openclaw update hint` — expect `UPGRADE_AVAILABLE` output
3. Invoke any skill (e.g. `$openclaw-pr-maintainer`) in Claude Code — expect the preamble to surface the hint

### Expected

- Silent when up-to-date, one-liner when update available
- `--json` always emits valid JSON on all paths

### Actual

- Matches expected behavior

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

13 unit tests pass covering: no state file, no version field, up-to-date, newer available, local-newer, corrupt JSON, non-string fields, non-object root (`null`, numeric), `--json` on all paths.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `openclaw update hint` with/without state file, with/without `--json`, with stale and current versions. Invoked skill in Claude Code and confirmed preamble surfaces the hint.
- Edge cases checked: corrupt JSON, `null` root, numeric root, non-string `lastAvailableVersion`, missing fields
- What you did **not** verify: Windows behavior (CI covers this), gateway write path (unchanged)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

### Review feedback addressed

- **Greptile P1 / Codex P2 (`--json` empty output):** all early-return paths now emit `{ "updateAvailable": false }` when `--json` is set (a313ac1)
- **Codex P2 (non-string field shapes):** `lastAvailableVersion` and `lastAvailableTag` guarded with `typeof === "string"` before `.trim()` (6a64813)
- **Codex P2 (non-object root shape):** parsed JSON root validated as a non-null, non-array object before accessing any fields (9ee09ca)
- **Greptile P2 (Zod):** Acknowledged as follow-up — code is already defensive with root-shape validation, typeof guards, optional chaining, and try/catch

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Preamble adds ~50ms latency to skill invocations
  - Mitigation: The command reads a single local file with no network calls. If the file is missing or corrupt, it exits immediately.

target.title: fix(msteams): isolate thread sessions, outbound targeting, and attachment resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #58615 — msteams channel threads share the same session key, causing cross-thread context bleed.

- **Thread session isolation:** Each Teams channel thread gets its own session key via `resolveThreadSessionKeys()`, gated by `session.threadBindings.enabled` config
- **Thread-scoped history:** `historyKey` is now thread-aware so `buildPendingHistoryContextFromMap` doesn't mix channel-level and thread-level history
- **Outbound thread targeting:** `buildActivity()` sets `activity.replyToId` before any early returns so proactive sends (text + file attachments via SharePoint/OneDrive) land in the correct thread
- **User mention preservation:** New `stripMSTeamsBotMentionTag()` strips only the bot's `<at>` tag — other user mentions are preserved as `@Name` in agent context. A separate `commandText` (all mentions stripped) is used for slash command detection to avoid regressions.
- **Key normalization:** Consistent `toLowerCase()` across session key, history key, and conversation store key derivations
- **Thread file attachment fix:** Graph API fallback now triggers for mixed attachment types (text/html + file.download.info without downloadUrl), fixing file attachments not being visible in thread replies. Narrowed to only file-like content types to avoid unnecessary Graph calls for Adaptive Cards.
- **Thread-aware debounce:** Debounce key includes `replyToId` when thread bindings enabled, preventing cross-thread message merging

## AI-Assisted PR 🤖

- [x] Marked as AI-assisted
- [x] Fully tested (487 msteams tests pass, type-check clean, lint clean, build clean)
- [x] Session logs available on request
- [x] I understand what the code does
- [x] Bot review conversations resolved with commit references

## Test plan

- [x] 487/487 msteams extension tests pass (44 test files)
- [x] Type-check (`pnpm tsgo`) clean
- [x] Lint/format (`pnpm check`) clean
- [x] Build (`pnpm build`) clean
- [x] New unit tests for `stripMSTeamsBotMentionTag` (10 cases)
- [x] New unit tests for `buildActivity` replyToId thread targeting (3 cases)
- [x] New integration tests for thread isolation logic (12 cases)
- [x] New unit tests for Graph API fallback on mixed attachment types (6 cases)
- [ ] Manual verification: proactive sends with `activity.replyToId` land in correct Teams thread
- [ ] Manual verification: file attachments in thread replies are visible to the bot

target.title: feat(backup): add `openclaw backup restore` command
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw has `backup create` and `backup verify` but no way to restore from a backup archive — users must manually extract tar.gz files and move them back into place.
- Why it matters: A restore command completes the backup lifecycle, making it safe for users to rely on backups before destructive operations like `reset` or `uninstall`.
- What changed: Added `openclaw backup restore <archive>` subcommand with `--dry-run`, `--force`, and `--json` options. Extracted shared archive-reading utilities from `backup-verify.ts` into a reusable `backup-archive-read.ts` module. Added `decodeAbsolutePathFromBackupArchive()` as the inverse of the existing path encoder.

target.title: [codex] Docs: add Agent Computer hosting guide
target.body:
This adds a new OpenClaw hosting guide for Agent Computer and wires that provider into the main OpenClaw docs discovery surfaces.

Agent Computer is Companion's managed-worker platform for browser-accessible cloud machines:
- https://www.agentcomputer.ai

For this integration, the relevant user entrypoint is the OpenClaw shortcut on Agent Computer:
- https://www.agentcomputer.ai/openclaw

Before this change, the OpenClaw hosting docs covered providers such as exe.dev, Fly.io, Hetzner, and Azure, but there was no equivalent page for Agent Computer. That meant reviewers and users had no first-party OpenClaw doc explaining how to run OpenClaw there, even though Agent Computer is now a real browser-first path for creating an OpenClaw machine.

The main product difference versus a VM-first host such as exe.dev is that Agent Computer already owns the machine lifecycle and browser surface. OpenClaw runs inside that managed worker and is exposed through a published port instead of taking over the machine's canonical host. In practice, that means the guide must explicitly switch OpenClaw away from loopback-only binding and publish port 18789 over HTTPS.

This PR adds `docs/install/agentcomputer.md` and updates the primary hosting indexes so Agent Computer appears as a one-click browser setup option near the top of the relevant provider lists. It also adds the Mintlify navigation/redirect entries in `docs/docs.json` and the glossary entry needed for the new page title.

Scope in this PR:
- `docs/install/agentcomputer.md`
- `docs/install/index.md`
- `docs/vps.md`
- `docs/platforms/index.md`
- `docs/help/faq.md`
- `docs/docs.json`
- `docs/.i18n/glossary.zh-CN.json`

Validation for this docs-only change was limited to the documentation gates:
- `pnpm format` passed
- `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` passed
- Open review-thread feedback was addressed and resolved

I also ran `pnpm check` after refreshing dependencies with `pnpm install`; that broader repo gate still failed on unrelated existing TypeScript errors in `extensions/telegram/src/bot.ts` around `AbortSignal` typing, which is outside the scope of this docs PR.

target.title: Add user chat bubble color selector for macOS application
target.body: # Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: The macOS chat bubble color is hardcoded to a static blue, with no way for users to personalize it. - Why it matters: Users want visual customization of the chat interface to match their preferences or distinguish between instances. - What changed: Added a new "Colors" settings tab with a 10-color palette picker, a custom `ColorPicker`, and a system accent color toggle. `ColorPreferencesStore` (in `OpenClawKit`) persists preferences via `UserDefaults` and exposes a `resolvedColor` that `ChatTheme.userBubble` now reads on macOS. `userText` dynamically picks black or white based on WCAG relative luminance. A cross-platform `ColorHexSupport` utility was added to `OpenClawKit` for hex-to-Color and Color-to-hex conversions. The macOS-local `ColorHexSupport` gained a reverse `hex(from:)` helper. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): iOS chat bubble color remains the existing static blue. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - macOS: New "Colors" tab in Settings (between "Channels" and "Skills") with three selection modes: system accent color (default, preserves existing behavior), palette color (10 curated colors from Apple's Human Interface Guidelines), or arbitrary custom color via `ColorPicker`. - Chat bubble color updates live when a new color is selected; preferences persist across app restarts via `UserDefaults`. - User message text automatically switches between white and black depending on the chosen bubble color's luminance (WCAG threshold at 0.6). ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: [user message] -> [hardcoded rgb(127,184,212) bubble] -> [white text] After: [user message] -> [ColorPreferencesStore.resolvedColor] -> [palette / custom / accent bubble] -> [WCAG luminance > 0.6?] -> yes -> [black text] -> no -> [white text] ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS 15+ - Runtime/container: Swift 6 / SwiftUI - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Build and launch the macOS app. 2. Open Settings > Colors. 3. Select a palette color, apply a custom color, or toggle "Use Accent Color." 4. Open a chat session see the selected color on your messages. ### Expected - User chat bubble reflects the selected color. - Text is white on dark bubbles and black on light bubbles (e.g. yellow). - Preference survives app restart. ### Actual - Verified as expected. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) **Color palette** **Custom color** **Dark text on bright color** ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Palette selection, custom color via ColorPicker, accent color toggle, preference persistence across restart, live bubble color update in chat. - Edge cases checked: Light colors (yellow, orange) produce black text; dark colors (indigo, blue) produce white text. Clearing custom color falls back to palette or accent. Off-main-thread `resolvedColor` defaults to light palette variant. - What you did **not** verify: iOS runtime (feature is macOS-only for now). No automated Swift tests were added for the new views or store. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: `ColorPreferencesStore.shared` is `nonisolated(unsafe)` and initialized eagerly in `AppState.init`; concurrent first access from a background thread could race. - Mitigation: `AppState` calls `_ = ColorPreferencesStore.shared` during init on the main actor, ensuring the singleton is created before any background reader. - Risk: `UserDefaults`-backed preferences do not sync across macOS reinstalls or profiles. - Mitigation: Defaults to system accent color (existing behavior); preferences are non-critical and easily re-set.

target.title: docs(whatsapp): clarify remote QR pairing limitations
target.body:
## Summary
Document the practical limitations of the current QR-only WhatsApp login flow in remote/headless setups.

## Why
We hit a real-world failure mode where:
- OpenClaw WhatsApp login was QR-only
- the target machine was not physically nearby
- relaying the QR through screenshots / PDFs / chat attachments was brittle
- the QR refreshed or expired underneath the handoff

This does not add new behavior. It makes the limitation explicit in the channel docs and nudges users toward a direct QR image handoff plan instead of assuming terminal capture will be fine.

## Notes
- This PR does **not** claim phone-number / pairing-code login is available in the current documented flow.
- Related issues:
  - #45652
  - #56797

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared. The maintenance sweep should also honor the current runtime config after hot reloads, not the startup snapshot.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed. The initial scheduler also captured the startup config, which meant hot reloads could leave the sweep using stale reset policy until restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - A stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message.
  - A fresh daily session is left alone.
  - Implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are not cut by the default daily policy.
  - Sessions with active runs are skipped.
  - Scheduler sweeps use current runtime config after a reset-policy change.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution or reload-aware maintenance behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary. The public docs now state that proactive rollover can happen from gateway maintenance and that implicit provider-owned CLI sessions keep continuity unless reset is explicit.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> read current runtime config
  -> skip active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.
5. Mark a stale session key active and confirm it is skipped.
6. Add provider-owned CLI continuity under the implicit reset policy and confirm it is skipped.
7. Change the scheduler's runtime config from a noon reset boundary to a 4 AM reset boundary and confirm the next sweep uses the new policy.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh, active, and implicitly provider-owned CLI sessions remain unchanged; config reloads affect future scheduler sweeps.

### Actual

The focused scheduler and gateway maintenance tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused scheduler and maintenance test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.
- Docs updated in:
  - `docs/concepts/session.md`
  - `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`
  - `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, runtime-config reload path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer; type gates pass.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
- Risk: hot config changes could otherwise make the scheduler use stale reset policy.
- Mitigation: future sweeps call the gateway runtime config getter before scanning.
